entries.title: CORS Autofix
entries.notes: Intercepts CORS failures in network panel, identifies the missing Access-Control-* header, and either auto-injects a browser-level proxy rule or generates the exact server config snippet. One engineer can build the header-matching logic and config generator in under a week. CORS is still the #1 network pain point for junior devs and API integrators.
entries.pain: CORS errors waste hours of debugging time and require tedious header hunting across docs.

entries.title: Outreach Copilot
entries.notes: Scrapes the current LinkedIn profile or company page in the background and drafts a personalized opening line, relevant talking point, and CTA in under 5 seconds. Buildable with a simple LLM wrapper and page scraping. The bridge from free browser tool to paid SaaS CRM plugin is natural. Cold outreach burnout is real and getting worse.
entries.pain: Sales reps spend 20 minutes researching each prospect before writing one cold email.

entries.title: Fingerprint Revealer
entries.notes: Runs a lightweight fingerprint probe (canvas, WebGL, audio, fonts, timezone) and shows a visual fingerprint heatmap with uniqueness score. Can be built with vanilla JS in a weekend. People are increasingly privacy-curious post-GDPR but have no tools to quantify their exposure. Tip jar works because the insight is surprising enough to trigger small payments.
entries.pain: Users have no idea how uniquely identifiable they are across the web.

entries.title: JSON Path Explorer
entries.notes: Injects a side panel that lists all JSON keys in a tree view and lets you click to filter, search, and export paths. Reads from the last network response automatically. The free tier handles basic tree view; paid tier adds path history and team sharing. Devtools audience is underserved for non-network-panel tasks.
entries.pain: Debugging nested JSON API responses requires guessing field names with no visual feedback.

entries.title: Font Audit Tool
entries.notes: Scans the DOM for font-size, word count, and paragraph density to calculate a real reading time that penalizes heavy formatting. Also scores readability (Flesch-Kincaid variant). Built with a simple DOM parser. Tip jar is natural for blog readers who want to share the tool. Lightweight and non-intrusive.
entries.pain: People waste time on bloated articles that could be skimmed in 2 minutes.

entries.title: Tech Stack Sniffer
entries.notes: One-click button that reveals CMS, analytics, CDN, frameworks, and hosting from script tags and meta headers. Builds a panel overlay with a confidence score per detection. Quota-based freemium (10 free scans/day, paid for power users). Directly replaces a workflow of 3-4 separate tools with one click.
entries.pain: Sales development reps manually check WhatCMS and builtwith to research prospect tech stacks.

entries.title: Redirect Chain Visualizer
entries.notes: Fetches the full redirect chain on click and renders it as a vertical timeline with status codes, headers, and timing per hop. Flags 301/302 loops and suspicious redirect patterns. Chrome DevTools doesn't expose this cleanly; competing tools like HTTPStatus.io require leaving the browser. Per-seat B2B pricing for agency and QA teams.
entries.pain: Debugging redirect chains means clicking through each hop and manually noting status codes.

entries.title: Privacy Request Generator
entries.notes: Select a website, pick which rights (access, deletion, opt-out), and generates a properly formatted email with legal citations auto-filled. Uses a simple template engine with jurisdiction-aware language. The privacy-conscious audience already wants to act but hits friction. Tip jar for users who find it valuable repeatedly.
entries.pain: Exercising GDPR/CCPA rights requires navigating legal jargon and filling out different forms per company.

entries.title: Form Field Spy
entries.notes: Auto-scans all forms on a page and outputs a structured table: field name, type, required flag, pattern, min/max, and observed error messages. Exportable as JSON or Markdown. Developers and QA teams share these manually in spreadsheets today. Freemium tier handles single-page scans; paid adds bulk site crawling.
entries.pain: QA testers and developers manually document every form field, its validation rules, and error states.

entries.title: Company Signal Aggregator
entries.notes: Fetches funding news, employee growth, recent job postings, and technology signals from the current company domain and displays a compact card overlay. Uses Clearbit-like enrichment APIs behind the scenes. Quota-based with a generous free tier (5 looks/day). Consolidates a 4-tab workflow into one click inside the browser.
entries.pain: Sales reps toggle between Crunchbase, LinkedIn, and news tabs to build prospect context.
